chapter: Chapter 2: Is Matter Around Us Pure

questions.question: Which of the following is a pure substance?
questions.explanation: Iron is an element, consisting of only one type of atom, making it a pure substance.

questions.question: A mixture of salt and sugar is an example of a:
questions.explanation: Salt and sugar particles do not mix uniformly at a molecular level and can be physically distinguished.

questions.question: The component of a solution that is dissolved in the liquid is called the:
questions.explanation: In a solution, the substance being dissolved is the solute, while the dissolving medium is the solvent.

questions.question: Which of the following is a homogeneous mixture?
questions.explanation: A salt solution has a uniform composition throughout, so it is homogeneous.

questions.question: The scattering of light by colloidal particles is known as:
questions.explanation: The Tyndall effect is the phenomenon of light scattering by particles in a colloid or a very fine suspension.

questions.question: Which of the following is a physical change?
questions.explanation: Melting of wax is a change of state; the chemical composition of wax remains the same.

questions.question: A solution that cannot dissolve any more solute at a given temperature is called a:
questions.explanation: A saturated solution contains the maximum amount of solute that can be dissolved at that temperature.

questions.question: Tincture of iodine is a solution of iodine in:
questions.explanation: Tincture of iodine is iodine (solute) dissolved in alcohol (solvent).

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an alloy?
questions.explanation: Brass is an alloy (homogeneous mixture) of Copper and Zinc.

questions.question: The size of particles in a true solution is less than:
questions.explanation: True solution particles are smaller than 1 nanometer (10^-9 m) in diameter.

questions.question: Which technique is used to separate cream from milk?
questions.explanation: Centrifugation is used for separating denser and lighter particles in a mixture when spun rapidly.

questions.question: Brass is a mixture of approximately 30% zinc and 70% ______.
questions.explanation: Brass is composed primarily of copper and zinc.

questions.question: A mixture of sulfur and iron filings can be separated by using:
questions.explanation: Iron is magnetic and can be pulled away from non-magnetic sulfur filings.

questions.question: A heterogeneous mixture in which the solute particles do not dissolve but remain suspended is a:
questions.explanation: In a suspension, particles are large enough to be seen and eventually settle down.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a metalloid?
questions.explanation: Iron is a metal. Boron, Silicon, and Germanium are metalloids (elements with intermediate properties).

questions.question: Air is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: Air is a homogeneous mixture containing about 78% Nitrogen and 21% Oxygen.

questions.question: Burning of a candle involves:
questions.explanation: Melting of wax is physical; burning of the wick and wax to produce CO2 and water is chemical.

questions.question: Blood and sea water are both:
questions.explanation: Both contain various different substances physically mixed together.

questions.question: Which of the following elements is a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury (metal) and Bromine (non-metal) are the only elements that are liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: What type of mixture is fog?
questions.explanation: Fog is an aerosol, which is a colloid of liquid (water) in gas (air).

questions.question: Compounds can be broken down into elements by:
questions.explanation: Compounds are chemically bonded and require chemical processes to break them apart.

questions.question: When salt is added to water, the water is the:
questions.explanation: Water is the dissolving medium, hence it is the solvent.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-metal?
questions.explanation: Iodine is a non-metal; the others are metals.

questions.question: Shaving cream is an example of:
questions.explanation: Shaving cream is a colloid where gas is dispersed in a liquid (Foam).

questions.question: Malleability and ductility are properties of:
questions.explanation: Metals can be hammered into sheets (malleable) and drawn into wires (ductile).
